PyTorch torch.bernoulli does not accept a 'p' parameter; it only takes 'input' (probabilities tensor). Passing 'p=0.3' to torch.bernoulli is invalid — correct usage requires input tensor with values 0.3, not scalar p. Paddle's 'p' param is optional and overrides input probabilities, but PyTorch has no equivalent — so to align semantics, we must generate an input tensor filled with 0.3, and remove 'p' from both APIs.
